programmes.show_description: The National Symphony Orchestra, Ireland perform Mozart's Piano Concerto no 20, the Irish premiere of Cindy McTee's 'Timepiece' and Richard Strauss's 'Ein Heldenleben'.
Linton Stephens presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Georgia Mann plays the best in classical music, with discoveries and surprises rubbing shoulders with familiar favourites.
Al Ryan introduces a concert recording from Munich of Charpentier's Marian mass and brings us highlights from the recent Belfast International Festival of Chamber Music.
4/5. Kate Molleson delves into the iconic Fifth Symphony by Jean Sibelius - and its famed programmatic depiction of swans.
Danny Driver plays live in the studio and Musical Director of the Bach Choir, David Hill.
Take time out with a 30-minute soundscape of classical music.
The BBC National Orchestra of Wales and Ryan Bancroft perform Hillborg's Exquisite Corpse, Shostakovich's 2nd Cello Concerto with Alban Gerhardt and Sibelius's Lemminkäinen Suite.

programmes.show_name: The Essay: You, Me and the Wireless
programmes.show_description: Legendary broadcaster Gillian Reynolds shares her life and career in radio, spanning over 50 years. The role of politics on air is her theme in this essay.
Hannah Peel presents an adventurous, immersive soundtrack for late-night listening, from classical to contemporary and everything in between.
Tonight, Melissa Aldana has one more bunch of flowers to give to a jazz artist that she appreciates.
Violinist Carolin Widmann and pianist Gabriele Carcano play sonatas by Schumann, Brahms and Fauré at the Pau Casals International Music Festival in Spain. Penny Gore presents.
Linton Stephens presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Georgia Mann plays the best in classical music, with discoveries and surprises rubbing shoulders with familiar favourites.
Al Ryan reaches the climax of his week focused on performances of distinctive settings of the mass with Beethoven, and he also casts an eye on festive chamber music from Belfast.
5/5. Kate Molleson follows Sibelius in the creation of his Sixth and Seventh Symphonies - and also the Eighth which the composer destroyed.
Harpist Anne-Sophie Bertrand and rediscovered pieces by Ethel Yeats and Augusta Zuckermann, who was known by her pseudonym Mana-Zucca.
Take time out with a 30-minute soundscape of classical music with music from Saint-Saens, Mendelssohn, Mozart and Howard Shore.
The BBC National Orchestra of Wales, conductor Ian McMillan-Davidson, and three vocal stars celebrate the music of Ivor Novello on the 75th anniversary of his death.

programmes.show_name: The Essay: You, Me and the Wireless
programmes.show_description: Legendary broadcaster Gillian Reynolds shares her life and career in radio, spanning over 50 years. Audio drama takes centre stage in this final essay.
Join Jennifer Lucy Allan for a celebration of the accordion's more experimental sides, from extended techniques to pedal manipulation.
Soweto presents concert highlights from vocalist, violinist, and friend of the show Alice Zawadzki, who blends different music traditions and jazz.
